macro_segments.title: The Generation of the Flood: Noah's Ark and the Prohibition of Intercourse

micro_segments.title: The meaning of 'husband and wife' for animals on the ark
Noah's knowledge of which animals had not sinned; the gate test
Alternative view: animals came of their own accord
The type of wood (gofer/mavliga/gulmish) for the ark
The tzohar (window/jewel) for light in the ark
Design of the ark: slanted roof and three levels
Three-level distribution: dung, animals, humans
Noah sent the raven; the raven's argument about being hated
Noah's response: prohibition of intercourse on the ark
Three who violated this: dog, raven, and Ham
Noah sent the dove; its preference for bitter sustenance and dependence on God
The dove as metaphor for the Jewish people
Animals came out by their families; unclear teaching on this

macro_segments.title: Life on the Ark: Testimonies of Shem and the Care of Animals

micro_segments.title: Eliezer questions Shem about conditions on the ark
Shem's account: round-the-clock feeding of animals
The chameleon: discovering what it eats through pomegranate
The lion: sustained by fever instead of food
The phoenix: never dies, receives blessing from Noah
Shem's counter-question: Eliezer's battles with the kings of east and west
Eliezer's account: Abraham, dirt-to-swords miracle, and conquest

macro_segments.title: Nachum Ish Gamzu and the Perfume Crown: Gam Zu Letovah

micro_segments.title: Nachum Ish Gamzu's practice of saying 'gam zu letovah'
Jewish people send Nachum with a precious crown to the Caesar
Thieves at the inn steal the crown and replace it with dirt
Nachum presents dirt to Caesar; Caesar suspects mockery
Eliyahu suggests the dirt is from Abraham's magic
Testing the dirt: it conquers an unconquerable city
Nachum rewarded with gold; innkeepers attempt to replicate success

macro_segments.title: The Generation of the Dispersion: Tower of Babel and Punishment

micro_segments.title: Dor HaPlaga has no share in Olam HaBa; what did they do?
Building a tower to reach the heavens and attack God
Objection: why build in a valley if aiming for heaven?
Three groups with different motivations for the tower
Group 1: wanting to dwell there; punishment was scattering
Group 2: wanting to wage war against God; made subhuman
Group 3: wanting to worship idols; punishment of language confusion
Rabbi Natan's unified view: all intended idolatry
Fate of the tower: burned, swallowed, and still standing
Why the tower is not found: air around it causes forgetting
Bavel and Borsif as bad omens for Torah

macro_segments.title: The People of Sodom: Sins and Systematic Wickedness

micro_segments.title: Anshei Sedom have no share in Olam HaBa
Two interpretations of 'wicked and sinners': body vs. money
Sexual sins and sins against the poor
Sedom's arrogance due to abundance: bread and gold
Prevention of passersby; diversion of river as punishment
Rabba's drasha: pushing wealthy people into leaning walls and robbery
Rabba's drasha: tunneling into homes using perfume markers
Rabbi Yose in Tzipori teaches the tunnel technique; 300 thieves emerge
Death of Rabbi Yose; blood flows in streets of Tzipori

macro_segments.title: Sodom's Perverse Laws and Justice System

micro_segments.title: Ox-watching tax: one day for owners, two days for non-owners
Orphan kills oxen; reverses logic of payment
Bridge toll and water path toll: differential taxation
Four judges with perverted names and practices
Forcing wife to another man for pregnancy; donkey ear compensation
Launderer beaten; must pay assailant as bloodletter

macro_segments.title: Eliezer's Encounters in Sodom and the Bed of Procrustes

micro_segments.title: The bed of Sodom: cutting off legs or stretching to fit
Eliezer refuses the guest bed by claiming an oath
Poor person given dinars by all; no one sells him food
Poor person dies; donors take back coins by name
Law against uninvited guests at weddings: stripper penalty
Eliezer crashes wedding, pins invitation on neighbors
Young woman hides bread in jug for poor; killed with honey and bees
Her death explained as fulfillment of 'outcry of Sodom'

macro_segments.title: Korah's Congregation and the Ten Tribes: Final Judgments

micro_segments.title: Adat Korach: in this world swallowed by earth, next world lost from congregation
Ten tribes exiled to another land; lost from congregation
Rabbi Akiva's view: ten tribes will be redeemed in future
Rabbi Eliezer: God brings death and life; can revive the tribes
Rabbi Yehuda ben Beteira: Korach congregation like lost thing God seeks

Sexual sin and abuse
Oppression of the poor and theft
Adat Korach (Korah's congregation) and punishment

Olam HaBa (World to Come) and share in it
Midah keneged midah (measure for measure)
Midrashic interpretation and drasha
Raven and its punishment: spitting
Dove's preference for bitterness and God-dependence

Language confusion at Babel (balal lashon)
